title: PHP while Loop
content: ### PHP while Loop The `while` loop executes a block of code as long as a condition is true. ### Syntax ```php ``` ### Example ```php "; $i++; } ?> ``` ### Common Use Cases | Use Case | Description | |----------|-------------| | Reading files | Loop until end of file | | Database results | Fetch until no more rows | | User input validation | Repeat until valid input | | Counter loops | When end condition is known | > **Key Point:** Be careful of infinite loops. Always ensure the condition can become false. If the condition is initially false, the loop body never executes.
codeExample: "; $i++; } // Practical: sum 1 to 10 $sum = 0; $n = 1; while ($n <= 10) { $sum += $n; $n++; } echo "Sum: $sum"; ?>

quiz.question: When does a while loop stop executing?
What happens if the while condition is false from the start?
